perturbation.original_text: Grantee shall pay to Grantor a Franchise Fee which shall be calculated pursuant to this Section. It is understood that Grantee has or will enter into franchise agreements with the City of Roanoke ("Roanoke") and the City of Salem ("Salem") and the Town of Vinton ("Vinton") (Grantor, Roanoke and Salem being hereinafter sometimes collectively referred to as the "localities" and singularly as a "locality") with fee provisions identical to this one, and that the total annual Franchise Fee to be paid to the three localities in aggregate is $98,196 for calendar year 2016 ("base year total annual Franchise Fee"). Grantor's Franchise Fee shall be a percentage share of the base year total annual Franchise Fee, which shall be determined on a pro rata basis according to its percentage share of the total dollar value of Grantee's gas sales occurring within the localities during the calendar year. For each calendar year of the Franchise, each locality's percentage share shall be determined by the following formula: total dollar value of Grantee's gas sales within Locality's percentage share = the Territorial Limits of the locality total dollar value of Grantee's gas sales in the three localities For calendar year 2016, the Franchise Fee shall be paid to Grantor on or before March 31, 2017.
perturbation.changed_text: Grantee shall pay to Grantor a Franchise Fee which shall be calculated pursuant to this Section. It is understood that Grantee has or will enter into franchise agreements with the City of Roanoke ("Roanoke") and the City of Salem ("Salem") and the Town of Vinton ("Vinton") (Grantor, Roanoke and Salem being hereinafter sometimes collectively referred to as the "localities" and singularly as a "locality") with fee provisions identical to this one, and that the total annual Franchise Fee to be paid to the three localities in aggregate will be determined at a later date. Grantor's Franchise Fee shall be a percentage share, which shall be determined on a pro rata basis according to its percentage share of the total dollar value of Grantee's gas sales occurring within the localities during the calendar year. For each calendar year of the Franchise, each locality's percentage share shall be determined by the following formula: total dollar value of Grantee's gas sales within Locality's percentage share = the Territorial Limits of the locality total dollar value of Grantee's gas sales in the three localities For calendar year 2016, the Franchise Fee shall be paid to Grantor on or before March 31, 2017.
perturbation.explanation: By omitting the specific amount for the “base year total annual Franchise Fee” and replacing it with 'will be determined at a later date,' a significant ambiguity is introduced. This omission creates uncertainty and potential disputes because the core financial obligation isn't clearly defined. The formula for calculating locality share is retained. The contradiction arises because the localities will calculate different Franchise Fee base amount, and Grantor will not know what that base amount is.
perturbation.contradicted_text: For each succeeding calendar year during the term of this Franchise, the total annual Franchise Fee paid by Grantee to the localities shall be the base year total annual Franchise Fee increased by three (3) percent compounded annually over the term of the Franchise. For each calendar year during the term of this Franchise, Grantor's percentage share shall be determined pursuant to this Section, and paid to Grantor on or before March 31 of the succeeding calendar year.
perturbation.justification: The changed text omits the base year total annual Franchise Fee, replacing it with 'will be determined at a later date.' This directly contradicts the 'Contradicted Text,' which states that 'For each succeeding calendar year during the term of this Franchise, the total annual Franchise Fee paid by Grantee to the localities shall be the base year total annual Franchise Fee increased by three (3) percent compounded annually.' Without a defined base year fee, the subsequent years' fees cannot be calculated, creating a clear and direct conflict within the document as described in Perturbation Explanation and the definition for Omissions.

perturbation.original_text: Grantee shall during the term of this Franchise maintain at least one business office within the Territorial Limits of Grantor. Such office shall be open at least forty (40) hours per week for the conduct of business between Grantee and its customers.
perturbation.changed_text: Grantee shall during the term of this Franchise maintain a presence within the Territorial Limits of Grantor. The details of this presence will be determined based on mutual agreement and operational needs.
perturbation.explanation: By changing the requirement to maintain a business office open for specific hours to a more vague 'presence,' the contract becomes ambiguous. This omission creates a contradiction, because the original text gave specifics on what the Grantee had to do, while the changed text is now giving vague terminology on what they are now required to do. This could allow Grantee to simply claim an occasional visit fulfills this vague requirement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Grantee shall during the term of this Franchise maintain a presence within the Territorial Limits of Grantor. The details of this presence will be determined based on mutual agreement and operational needs.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces ambiguity regarding the Grantee's obligations by replacing a specific requirement (business office open for specific hours) with a vague 'presence.' This contradicts the original intent of the contract to ensure accessible service and creates a loophole where the Grantee could argue minimal activity fulfills the requirement.

perturbation.original_text: All notices required under this Agreement or the Ordinance shall be in writing and shall be deemed validly given, unless otherwise required, when sent by certified mail, return receipt requested, or by a nationally recognized overnight courier, addressed as follows (or any other address the party to be notified may have designated to the sender by like notice): Grantor: Grantee: Town of Vinton Roanoke Gas Company Attention: Town Manager Attention: President 311 S. Pollard Street 519 Kimball Avenue, N.E. Vinton, Virginia 24179 P.O. Box 13007 Roanoke, Virginia 24030
perturbation.changed_text: All notices required under this Agreement or the Ordinance shall be in writing and shall be deemed validly given when sent to the parties. Further details of how to send notice will be determined at a later date.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the specific addresses and methods for sending notices (certified mail, courier), and replacing it with a statement that 'Further details of how to send notice will be determined at a later date', the contract becomes unenforceable regarding notices until such 'later date' arrives, because the addresses and methods has been omitted. This creates a contradiction because the first sentence is saying that all notices need to be in writing and sent to the parties, but there's no clear way on how to do so.
perturbation.contradicted_text: All notices required under this Agreement or the Ordinance shall be in writing and shall be deemed validly given when sent to the parties. Further details of how to send notice will be determined at a later date.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes specific instructions for sending notices (certified mail, addresses) and replaces them with a placeholder stating that details will be determined later. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which implies that notices should be given as long as they're sent to the parties, by making it impossible to determine how to send a valid notice until the 'later date' arrives. Since methods of contact are omitted, sending a notice becomes impossible and unenforceable, creating a strong in-text contradiction as per the 'Omissions – In Text Contradiction' definition.
